summary: llm-context-compressor exfiltrates host and filesystem data to hardcoded endpoints
details: The package ships lib/trap-core.js — a file whose name itself signals trap/beacon behavior — that imports fs, os, and https and performs multiple POST requests carrying host identity fields (hostname, platform) collected via os.hostname() and os.platform(). Multiple POST sinks (lines 383, 409, 464, 546, 547) send structured payloads that include hostname:, platform:, and filesystem-existence probes (fs.existsSync at lines 26, 79, 194). The combination of host identity collection, filesystem reconnaissance, and outbound HTTPS POSTs in a file explicitly named 'trap-core' is consistent with a credential/host beaconing payload rather than a legitimate context-compression library. There is no documented purpose for an LLM context compressor to enumerate the filesystem and beacon host metadata to remote endpoints.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
